---
title: "Object Reference"
sidebar: api
---

This is a conceptual reference for the proposed model objects.

| Object | Role |
|---|---|
| `Inventory` | Top-level catalog, manifest envelope, validation boundary, and replacement entrypoint. |
| `CreationInfo` | Provenance for the inventory document. |
| `Network` | FDSN-like organization level containing fiber arrays. |
| `FiberArray` | Station-like identity for an extended fiber observing system. |
| `Acquisition` | Time-aware acquisition setup and channel-to-distance context. |
| `Interrogator` | DAS interrogator hardware metadata. |
| `OpticalPath` | Ordered optical-distance path with parallel metadata tracks. |
| `FiberSegment` | Fiber interval within an optical path. |
| `Connector`, `Splice`, `Coupler`, `Turnaround`, `Terminator` | Common optical components. |
| `Geometry` | Coordinates along an optical-distance interval. |
| `CoordinateReferenceSystem` | CRS shared by path geometries. |
| `CouplingCondition` | Acoustic coupling context along an interval. |
| `OpticalPathAnnotation` | Label or category over a path distance range. |
| `Cable` | Reusable physical cable metadata. |
| `ExternalResource` | Reference to external documents, registries, product pages, or specifications. |

## Minimal Object Graph

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  Interrogator --> Acquisition
  Cable --> FiberSegment
  CRS[CoordinateReferenceSystem] --> Geometry
  FiberSegment --> OpticalPath
  Geometry --> OpticalPath
  CouplingCondition --> OpticalPath
  Acquisition --> FiberArray
  OpticalPath --> FiberArray
  FiberArray --> Network
  Network --> Inventory
```

## Naming Conventions

The proposal favors domain names that are useful in analysis:

- `FiberArray` instead of stretching point-station terminology over an extended aperture.
- `Acquisition` for channel-like acquisition context.
- `OpticalPath` for the ordered path followed by light.
- `data_source_id` for the patch-to-inventory join key.
